summary: Slowness in Claude Code is almost always context size, not a bug - use compaction not restart
problem: Responses feel slow so you restart Claude Code, losing all context, cache, and session state - and the next session is just as slow after a few turns
solution: Check context usage first. If it is above 60%, trigger compaction. The slowness is inference time scaling with context length, not a client bug. Restarting does not fix it and destroys your session state
root_cause: Inference latency scales with context window size; at 150K+ tokens, responses are genuinely slower. Compaction reduces active context and restores speed without losing work
